commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Protected maintainer handling is appropriate because this is a compatibility-sensitive Discord authorization boundary and the remaining blocker is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance, not an automated re...; Cleared: The diff adds no dependency, workflow, secret, or supply-chain changes and appears to tighten Discord authorization; the remaining concern is proof and compatibility review. (sha=d858762258db25478a0874a9701122ea69a7b626)
commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes Discord privileged guild-admin action detection, rejects cross-provider privileged Discord admin/moderation actions, preserves trusted owner/manual no-context Discord admin actions, and adds focused Discord handler plus runner tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +186. Total +204 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main only requires trusted sender metadata for Discord-origin tool contexts and the handler drops non-Discord requester identity, so the cross-provider privileged path is traceable without running tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer handling is appropriate because this is a compatibility-sensitive Discord authorization boundary and the remaining blocker is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance, not an automated repair task.

target.title: fix(discord): block cross-provider guild admin actions
target.body:
Summary

Fixes Discord guild-admin message action authorization so privileged Discord admin actions require a trusted Discord requester identity when invoked from another channel provider, while preserving trusted no-context owner/manual Discord admin actions.

Changes

- Centralize the Discord privileged guild-admin action list in a shared helper.
- Require trusted requester identity for privileged Discord actions in tool contexts before shared dispatch enters the Discord handler.
- Reject explicit non-Discord origins in the Discord guild-admin handler instead of dropping requester identity and continuing through a no-sender path.
- Preserve no-context owner/manual Discord guild-admin actions through the shared runner.
- Keep read-only Discord guild lookups available from non-Discord requesters.

Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cross-provider attempts to run privileged Discord guild-admin message actions are rejected unless they originate with a trusted Discord sender identity, and trusted owner/manual no-context Discord admin actions remain available.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `747` at committed head `d858762258db25478a0874a9701122ea69a7b626`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot` and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`.
Evidence after fix: The runner dispatch test passed with 1 test file and 23 tests, and the Discord extension lane passed with 158 test files and 1766 tests.
Observed result after fix: Committed regression coverage proves no-context owner Discord admin actions reach the plugin handler through `runMessageAction`, cross-provider privileged calls without a trusted sender are blocked by shared dispatch, cross-provider calls with a non-Discord trusted sender are rejected by the Discord handler, and read-only `channel-info` remains allowed from non-Discord contexts.
What was not tested: No live Discord API calls were run; validation used the existing Discord extension and runner/runtime test boundaries with no real credentials or Discord network calls.

Notes

- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
target.author: eleqtrizit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
